reason: All 6 remaining S2C keyslot candidates reviewed (P622-KS-001, -004, -005, -006, -009, -010) are confirmed to be VM handler/thunk blocks that only store register context on the native CPU stack (RSP) via PUSH instructions. No native heap or global key initialization code is present.
next_action: Document findings and stop. The S2C key state is a local VM context variable stored relative to RBP/RDI on the stack, meaning it cannot be recovered via static native memory writes in these dispatcher entries.
